topic: Search fusion weight optimization and reranking threshold calibration

findings.title: search-weights.json is secondary, not the main fusion tuning surface
findings.summary: The JSON file only feeds vector smart-ranking and no longer contains active RRF or cross-encoder configuration.

findings.title: Hybrid continuity precision is primarily controlled by adaptive weights plus RRF K
findings.summary: Hybrid fusion assembles weighted lists in hybrid-search using shared adaptive profiles, then applies shared RRF fusion.

findings.title: FSRS 0.2346 is a canonical invariant, not a packet-local tuning knob
findings.summary: The scheduler, cognitive docs, and search-time decay comments all encode the same FSRS v4 constant and formula.

findings.title: The repo already contains intent-specific RRF K optimization scaffolding
findings.summary: The analysis and tests sweep K across 10-120, with example expectations that literal lookup prefers low K while understand prefers high K.

findings.title: Cohere and Voyage improve first-hit quality over baseline in the project fixture corpus
findings.summary: Fixture evaluation indicates equivalent P@5 but higher RR@5 than baseline, with Cohere slightly ahead of Voyage on the fifth query.

findings.title: The long-document penalty mismatches the actual spec-doc corpus
findings.summary: The `>2000` threshold applies to the majority of markdown docs under `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit`, while the short threshold applies to none of them.

findings.title: Reranker cache hit rate is currently unobservable
findings.summary: The cache stores entries and exports latency samples but no hit/miss counters or TTL outcome metrics.

findings.title: Continuity queries should bias toward find_spec and find_decision profiles, not default weights
findings.summary: Normalized profiles show continuity-friendly graph weight between 0.23 and 0.45 with low recency, which aligns better with canonical source-of-truth retrieval than the default profile.
